content.text:
## Server lifecycle retention audit — `consul.Server` (`agent/consul/server.go:169`)

Method: `sense_blast`/`sense_graph` on `consul.Server` (composition + `retained_via_interfaces`), then every candidate verified against its actual construction site. Sense's interface pass proposed 31 rows; I confirmed the real ones, **rejected 6 false positives**, and found **one family of ~20 holders it missed entirely** (§F). Test-only holders excluded throughout.

---

### A. Direct `*Server` field — `agent/consul` package (31 holders)

Each keeps the concrete server for its whole life; all are built inside `NewServer` or its `register*` helpers and never re-pointed.

**Net-RPC endpoint receivers** (constructed in `agent/consul/server.go`, registered on `s.rpcServer`):

| Holder | Retaining field | Reaches server by |
|---|---|---|
| `ACL` | `agent/consul/acl_endpoint.go:108` | `srv *Server` |
| `Catalog` | `agent/consul/catalog_endpoint.go:79` | `srv *Server` |
| `Health` | `agent/consul/health_endpoint.go:24` | `srv *Server` |
| `KVS` | `agent/consul/kvs_endpoint.go:32` | `srv *Server` |
| `Session` | `agent/consul/session_endpoint.go:34` | `srv *Server` |
| `Txn` | `agent/consul/txn_endpoint.go:33` | `srv *Server` |
| `Internal` | `agent/consul/internal_endpoint.go:30` | `srv *Server` |
| `Coordinate` | `agent/consul/coordinate_endpoint.go:25` | `srv *Server`, plus its own batching goroutine |
| `Operator` | `agent/consul/operator_endpoint.go:10` | `srv *Server` |
| `Status` | `agent/consul/status_endpoint.go:15` | `server *Server` |
| `ConfigEntry` | `agent/consul/config_endpoint.go:26` | `srv *Server` |
| `Intention` | `agent/consul/intention_endpoint.go:43` | `srv *Server` |
| `DiscoveryChain` | `agent/consul/discovery_chain_endpoint.go:20` | `srv *Server` |
| `PreparedQuery` | `agent/consul/prepared_query_endpoint.go:45` | `srv *Server` |
| `ConnectCA` | `agent/consul/connect_ca_endpoint.go:47` | `srv *Server` |
| `AutoEncrypt` | `agent/consul/auto_encrypt_endpoint.go:18` | `srv *Server` |
| `FederationState` | `agent/consul/federation_state_endpoint.go:45` | `srv *Server` |

**Prepared-query failover wrappers** — created per query but retained for the call's duration:
- `stateLookup` — `agent/consul/prepared_query_endpoint.go:687` (`srv *Server`)
- `queryServerWrapper` — `agent/consul/prepared_query_endpoint.go:701` (`srv *Server`, built by `newQueryServerWrapper`, `:707`)

**Backends / adapters (the narrowing wrappers other packages hold — see §C/§D):**

| Holder | Retaining field | Built at |
|---|---|---|
| `serverACLResolverBackend` | `agent/consul/acl_server.go:112` (**embedded** `*Server`) | `agent/consul/server.go:633` |
| `caDelegateWithState` | `agent/consul/leader_connect_ca.go:91` (**embedded** `*Server`) | `agent/consul/server.go:664` |
| `PeeringBackend` | `agent/consul/peering_backend.go:33` | `NewPeeringBackend`, `:43` |
| `OperatorBackend` | `agent/consul/operator_backend.go:17` | `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:445` |
| `ConfigEntryBackend` | `agent/consul/configentry_backend.go:13` | `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:529` |
| `subscribeBackend` | `agent/consul/subscribe_backend.go:16` | `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:466` |
| `autoConfigBackend` | `agent/consul/auto_config_backend.go:20` | `agent/consul/server.go:1203` |
| `V1TenancyBridge` | `agent/consul/tenancy_bridge.go:12` | `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:344` |
| `v1ServiceExportsShim` | `agent/consul/v2_config_entry_exports_shim.go:24` | `agent/consul/server.go:879` |
| `raftHandle` | `agent/consul/raft_handle.go:21` | `agent/consul/server.go:527` |

**Long-running subsystems:**
- `AutopilotDelegate` — `agent/consul/autopilot.go:36` (`server *Server`); handed to the external `raft-autopilot` library, which retains it.
- `FederationStateReplicator` — `agent/consul/federation_state_replication.go:23` (`srv *Server`); wrapped in `IndexReplicator` at `agent/consul/server.go:608-612`.
- `CertExpirationMonitor` — `agent/consul/leader_metrics.go:127` (`Server *Server`); set only by `rootCAExpiryMonitor` (`:56`) and `signingCAExpiryMonitor` (`:75`). The agent-TLS variant at `agent/metrics.go:56` leaves it nil.

---

### B. The server retained through a locally-declared interface, inside `agent/consul`

These files declare their own narrow interface; the value that lands is the server or a §A wrapper.

- **`ACLResolver.backend`** — `agent/consul/acl.go:278` (type `ACLResolverBackend`). Set from `ACLResolverConfig.Backend` (`agent/consul/acl.go:160`), assigned `&serverACLResolverBackend{Server: s}` at `agent/consul/server.go:633`. *(The client path uses `clientACLResolverBackend` — not a server handle.)*
- **`CAManager.delegate`** — `agent/consul/leader_connect_ca.go:60` (type `caServerDelegate`), given `&caDelegateWithState{Server: s}` at `agent/consul/server.go:664`.
- **`ca.ConsulProvider.Delegate`** — `agent/connect/ca/provider_consul.go:34` (type `ConsulProviderStateDelegate`). Second hop: `CAManager` passes its own delegate on at `agent/consul/leader_connect_ca.go:462`, and retains the provider in `CAManager.provider`. **This handle is minted at leadership acquisition, not at construction** — the one holder your rework must re-wire on leader change, not just on restart.
- **`GatewayLocator.srv`** — `agent/consul/gateway_locator.go:44` (type `serverDelegate`), given `s` directly at `agent/consul/server.go:573-578`. Only created when `ConnectMeshGatewayWANFederationEnabled`; its `PickGateway` method is then installed into `s.connPool` and `s.grpcConnPool` (`:580-581`) — a callback edge that also outlives a naive swap.
- **`rate.Handler.serversStatusProvider`** — `agent/consul/rate/handler.go:164` (type `ServersStatusProvider`), set by `incomingRPCLimiter.Register(s)` at `agent/consul/server.go:525`.
- **`IndexReplicator.Delegate`** — `agent/consul/replication.go:246` (type `IndexReplicatorDelegate`), carrying `FederationStateReplicator` (§A) from `agent/consul/server.go:608`.
- **`reporting.ReportingManager.server`** — `agent/consul/reporting/reporting.go:17` (type `ServerDelegate`), given `s` at `agent/consul/server.go:786`.

---

### C. gRPC service configs holding the server (external + internal)

Each `Config` is embedded by value into its `Server`, so the field *is* the retaining field.

- **`peering.Config.FSMServer`** — `agent/rpc/peering/service.go:99` (type `blockingquery.FSMServer`) ← **`s` directly**, `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:433`. Also `peering.Config.Backend` (`:89`) ← `s.peeringBackend`.
- **`configentry.Config.FSMServer`** — `agent/grpc-external/services/configentry/server.go:38` ← **`s` directly**, `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:534`. Also `configentry.Config.Backend` (`:36`) ← `NewConfigEntryBackend(s)`, `:529`.
- **`peerstream.Config.Backend`** — `agent/grpc-external/services/peerstream/server.go:36` ← `s.peeringBackend`, `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:387`.
- **`peerstream.subscriptionManager.backend`** — `agent/grpc-external/services/peerstream/subscription_manager.go:46` (type `SubscriptionBackend`) ← `s.Backend` at `stream_resources.go:350`. Per-stream, so it can outlive a replaced server for the life of an open peer stream.
- **`peerstream.exportedServiceRequest.sub`** — `agent/grpc-external/services/peerstream/subscription_view.go:25` (type `Subscriber`) ← `m.backend` at `subscription_manager.go:750`.
- **`submatview.LocalMaterializerDeps.Backend`** — `agent/submatview/local_materializer.go:29` (type `LocalBackend`), retained via `LocalMaterializer.deps` (`:23`). Reaches `PeeringBackend` on the peerstream path only (`subscription_view.go:78-79`).
- **`operator.Config.Backend`** — `agent/rpc/operator/service.go:70` ← `NewOperatorBackend(s)`, `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:445`.
- **`resource.Config.TenancyBridge`** — `agent/grpc-external/services/resource/server_ce.go:30` ← `NewV1TenancyBridge(s)`, `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:344`, plumbed through `newResourceServiceConfig` (`agent/consul/server_ce.go:208`).
- **`subscribe.Server.Backend`** — `agent/grpc-internal/services/subscribe/subscribe.go:23` ← `&subscribeBackend{srv: s, ...}`, `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:466`.

**Closure-captured handles (no interface, no field type to grep — the invisible ones).** `aclgrpc.Config` at `agent/grpc-external/services/acl/server.go:20-31` holds *seven* function fields that all close over `s`: `LoadAuthMethod`, `NewLogin`, `NewTokenWriter`, `ForwardRPC`, `LocalTokensEnabled`, `ValidateEnterpriseRequest` (wired `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:356-372`). The same `ForwardRPC: func(...){ return s.ForwardGRPC(s.grpcConnPool, ...) }` closure is retained by peerstream (`:396`), peering (`:424`), operator (`:449`), connectca (`:487`), configentry (`:532`); `GetStore: func() { return s.FSM().State() }` by peerstream (`:388`), connectca (`:480`), dataplane (`:501`). **None of these appear in any interface-based scan.**

---

### D. Storage / controller layers outside `agent/`

- **`raft.Backend.handle`** — `internal/storage/raft/backend.go:80` (type `Handle`) ← `&raftHandle{s}`, `agent/consul/server.go:527`.
- **`raft.forwardingClient.handle`** — `internal/storage/raft/forwarding.go:115` (type `Handle`), copied from the backend in `newForwardingClient` (`:123`).
- **`v1compat.reconciler.config`** — `internal/multicluster/internal/controllers/v1compat/controller.go:81` (type `AggregatedConfig`) ← `v1ServiceExportsShim` via `agent/consul/server.go:879-880` → `multicluster/exports.go:24` → `controllers/register.go:16`. The shim's `EventChannel()` is also registered as a controller watch source (`v1compat/controller.go:78`).
- **`controllers.CompatDependencies.ConfigEntryExports`** — `internal/multicluster/internal/controllers/register.go:11` — the struct the above travels in.

---

### E. `agent` package (server-mode only)

- **`Agent.delegate`** — `agent/agent.go:249`, type `delegate` (declared `:153`). Assigned `consulServer` at `agent/agent.go:709` in server mode. This is the root agent-side handle; ~15 call sites type-assert back to `*consul.Server` (e.g. `:1668`, `:1790`, `:1865`, `:1927`).
- **`local.State.Delegate`** — `agent/local/state.go:178`, type `rpc` (declared `:163`). Assigned `a.delegate` at `agent/agent.go:762` — so in server mode the anti-entropy state holds the server. Comment there already flags the ordering hazard: *"It is set after both the state and the consul server/agent have been created."*

---

### F. `agent/proxycfg-glue` — the family the interface scan missed (server-mode data sources)

`ServerDataSourceDeps` (`agent/proxycfg-glue/glue.go:29`) is **not** an interface and never names `consul.Server`, but at `agent/agent.go:4653-4660` it is filled with `ACLResolver: a.delegate` (the `*consul.Server` itself, `:4658`) and `GetStore: func() { return server.FSM().State() }` (closure over the server, `:4659`). It is then **copied by value into 20 data-source structs**, each retaining a live server handle for the life of the proxy config watch:

`peering_list.go:33` · `federation_state_list_mesh_gateways.go:33` · `gateway_services.go:33` · `service_list.go:31` and `:48` · `health.go:45` and `:63` · `health_blocking.go:46` · `resolved_service_config.go:34` · `exported_peered_services.go:33` · `discovery_chain.go:37` · `peered_upstreams.go:32` · `service_http_checks.go:30` · `config_entry.go:44` and `:99` · `intentions.go:75` · `intention_upstreams.go:49` · `trust_bundle.go:35` and `:83` · `internal_service_dump.go:56`

Because the handle is a *copied struct containing a closure*, replacing the server leaves all 20 silently reading the dead server's FSM. Treat this as one re-wire point (`agent/agent.go:4653`) with 20 downstream copies.

---

### G. Candidates ruled out (Sense proposed these; construction sites disprove them)

Recording these so nobody re-audits them:

- `OverviewManager.stateProvider` (`agent/consul/server_overview.go:21`) ← `s.fsm`, **not** the server (`agent/consul/server.go:783`).
